name: Discrete-spacetime quantum gravity (CDT + causal sets + Horava-Lifshitz)
summary: Classification: TOE-ASPIRATIONAL (research-program-level lumping). Three approaches that share the structural commitment that spacetime is fundamentally discrete (or that its continuum limit is highly non-classical) and that the spectral dimension reduces to ~2 at the Planck scale: Causal Dynamical Triangulations (Ambjorn-Jurkiewicz-Loll path integral over discrete causally-foliated 4D triangulations), causal set theory (Bombelli-Lee-Meyer-Sorkin discrete partial-order substrate), and Horava-Lifshitz gravity (anisotropic UV scaling making gravity power-counting renormalisable). Ambjorn-Goerlich-Jurkiewicz-Loll (2013) argued CDT is the path-integral version of Horava's framework, justifying the lumping. The shared phenomenological signature is dimensional reduction at the Planck scale, observable in principle via Lorentz-violation tests for the Horava sector. Closes quantum-gravity-consistency by hypothesis; addresses dimensional-reduction-at-planck strongly; has no canonical matter sector and does not address SM parameters, dark matter, or baryogenesis within the program.

lineage.citation: Bombelli, Lee, Meyer, Sorkin, 'Space-time as a causal set', PRL 59, 521 (1987)
Ambjorn, Jurkiewicz, Loll, 'Reconstructing the universe', PRD 72, 064014 (2005)
Ambjorn, Jurkiewicz, Loll, 'Spectral dimension of the universe', PRL 95, 171301 (2005)
Horava, 'Quantum gravity at a Lifshitz point', PRD 79, 084008 (2009)
Ambjorn, Goerlich, Jurkiewicz, Loll, 'Quantum gravity via causal dynamical triangulations', in 'Springer Handbook of Spacetime' (2014)
Surya, 'The causal set approach to quantum gravity', Living Rev. Relativ. 22, 5 (2019)
